# Accounts Quick Start

When users sign in to your Varity app, they automatically get an account. No extra setup needed.

:::tip[How it works]
- Users sign in with email or social login
- An embedded wallet is created automatically behind the scenes via Privy
- No browser extensions or special apps required
:::

## Prerequisites

- A Varity project with authentication set up ([Auth Quick Start](/build/auth/quickstart))
- Node.js 18 or later

## What Happens When Users Sign In

When a user logs in via `PrivyStack`, Varity automatically:

1. Authenticates the user via email, social login, or wallet connection
2. Creates an embedded wallet linked to their identity (managed by Privy)
3. Sets up session handling

You don't need to configure anything extra -- `PrivyStack` handles everything.

## Checking Account Status

Use the `usePrivy` hook to check if a user is signed in and access their wallet:

```tsx title="src/components/AccountStatus.tsx"
import { usePrivy, useWallets } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

function AccountStatus() {
  const { authenticated, user, ready } = usePrivy();
  const { wallets } = useWallets();

  if (!ready) return <p>Loading...</p>;
  if (!authenticated) return <p>Not signed in</p>;

  const embeddedWallet = wallets.find(w => w.walletClientType === 'privy');

  return (
    <div>
      <p>Signed in as: {user?.email?.address || 'User'}</p>
      {embeddedWallet && (
        <p>Wallet: {embeddedWallet.address}</p>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Protecting Routes

Show content only to signed-in users:

```tsx title="src/components/ProtectedDashboard.tsx"
import { PrivyProtectedRoute } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

function App() {
  return (
    <PrivyProtectedRoute>
      <Dashboard />
    </PrivyProtectedRoute>
  );
}
```

## Sending Transactions

Authenticated users can send transactions through their Privy embedded wallet. Use the `useSendTransaction` hook from Privy:

```tsx title="src/components/SendAction.tsx"
import { usePrivy } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';
import { useSendTransaction } from '@privy-io/react-auth';

function SendAction() {
  const { authenticated } = usePrivy();
  const { sendTransaction } = useSendTransaction();

  const handleAction = async () => {
    const txResult = await sendTransaction({
      to: '0x...',
      data: '0x...',
    });
    console.log('Transaction hash:', txResult.hash);
  };

  return (
    <button onClick={handleAction} disabled={!authenticated}>
      Perform Action
    </button>
  );
}
```

<Aside type="note">
**Beta** -- All operations run on Varity's beta environment. Production launch coming soon.
</Aside>

<Accordion title="Advanced: Smart Account Infrastructure">

Varity's UI Kit includes a fully implemented `SmartWalletProvider` (630+ lines) that adds account abstraction features such as gasless transactions and batch operations. This component uses thirdweb v5 smart wallets internally.

**Current status:** The smart wallet source code exists in the `@varity-labs/ui-kit` package but is not yet exposed as a public export. It is being prepared for a future release. When available, it will enable:

- **Gasless transactions** -- Users never pay gas fees
- **Batch operations** -- Send multiple transactions at once
- **Smart account features** -- Programmable account logic

For now, use the Privy embedded wallet flow shown above. The `PrivyStack` provider and `usePrivy` / `useWallets` hooks are the recommended way to handle user accounts.

</Accordion>

## Available Hooks and Components

All of the following are exported from `@varity-labs/ui-kit`:

| Export | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `PrivyStack` | Component | Wraps your app with Privy authentication |
| `VarityPrivyProvider` | Component | Lower-level Privy provider |
| `PrivyLoginButton` | Component | Pre-built sign-in button |
| `PrivyUserProfile` | Component | Displays user profile info |
| `PrivyProtectedRoute` | Component | Protects routes from unauthenticated access |
| `PrivyReadyGate` | Component | Renders children only after Privy initializes |
| `usePrivy` | Hook | Access auth state, user data, login/logout |
| `useWallets` | Hook | Access connected wallets |
| `useLogin` | Hook | Programmatic login |
| `useLogout` | Hook | Programmatic logout |
